title: Windows 10 Style Taskbar always shows text as black
author: TroyWolbrink
body: ### Before reporting your issue - [x] I have confirmed that this issue does not happen when ExplorerPatcher is not installed - [x] I do not have "register as shell extension" enabled - [x] I have tried my best to check existing issues ### Repro ExplorerPatcher versions 26100.8457.70.1 26100.8457.70.4 Worked fine in 26100.4946.69.6_7384790. ### Repro Windows Versions Windows 11 Pro, 25H2, x64, 26200.8457 ### Third-party tweak software installed Open Shell 4.4.196 ### Describe the bug I'm on Windows 11 25H2, and using ExplorerPatcher to use the "Windows 10 (ExplorerPatcher)" style taskbar. The text of an application icon is always black. I've tried both dark and light mode. I've tried with and without classic theme mitigations. No matter what I do, the text is always black. Since I like a dark looking task bar, I'd like the text to be white. In the past I was using Open Shell to fix this. But starting with version '26100.8457.70.1', my Open Shell taskbar customizations are no longer respected. I've tried turning off Open Shell's "Customize Taskbar", but this makes no difference. ### Expected outcome I expected the text on my taskbar to be white, or really anything but always black. ### Actual outcome Text on taskbar is ALWAYS black. ### Additional info _No response_ ### Crash Dumps _No response_ ### Media _No response_

title: Explorerpatcher crashes 3rd party application as it tries to patch system DLLs
author: jb-wisemo
body:
### Before reporting your issue

- [x] I have confirmed that this issue does not happen when ExplorerPatcher is not installed
- [ ] I do not have "register as shell extension" enabled (disabling it is a workaround but looses EP functionality)
- [x] I have tried my best to check existing issues

### Repro ExplorerPatcher versions

ExplorerPatcher 26100.8457.70d.4 (also happened in .3)


### Repro Windows Versions

Win11 10.0.22631.6199

### Third-party tweak software installed

OpenShell for its start menu

### Describe the bug

Affected application PwSafe (64 bit) 3.72.1 from pwsafe.org

Observed in x64 WinDbg: process crashes when ExplorerPatcher tries to patch a shell related system dll loaded in the PwSafe.exe process.

In EP 10.0.22631.6199.4 this is in the call to VnPatchIAT(hShell32, "user32.dll", "TrackPopupMenu", TrackPopupMenuHook); where an attempt to zero out an IAT entry en SHELL32.dll crashes .
In EP 10.0.22631.6199.3 this was in an attempt to change byte 3 of an instruction in ExplorerFrame.DLL to 0x03 even though that byte was already 0x03

At this particular point in time, it doesn't look like PwSafe was about to display any Explorer-based UI, the DLL to patch just happened to be loaded as part of process startup.  I suspect this was triggered via the "Install as shell extension" feature

As a workaround, I suggest providing an advanced option to exclude a list of named processes from getting DLLs patched by ExplorerPatcher.
Alternatively always remember to VirtualProtect the destination of a patch write and be prepared for VirtualProtect() to fail.

### Expected outcome

Nothing happens and PwSafe opens normally.

### Actual outcome

PwSafe crashes (status C0000005).
Running PwSafe under WinDbg with the PE 26100.8457.70.4 source zip and PDB file downloaded shows a crash as ExplorerPatcher tries to patch a system DLL, specifically PE version 26100.8457.70.4 was trying to patch the IAT in SHELL32.DLL, while PE version 26100.8457.70.3 was trying to patch an instruction in ExplorerFrame.dll (PE debug info not downloaded for this build).

Also, the EP source zip appears to lack the source for libvalinet, but I recognize DLL patching code when I see it in the Disassembly window.

### Additional info

EP had the option to download and use symbol files enabled.


### Crash Dumps

Because PwSafe was loading secret information, crash dumps cannot be shared, but I have tried to describe the crash location seen.


### Media

_No response_

title: Tray application popup is partially hidden behind left-side auto-hidden taskbar
author: mtakacs8305
body: ### Before reporting your issue - [x] I have confirmed that this issue does not happen when ExplorerPatcher is not installed - [x] I do not have "register as shell extension" enabled - [x] I have tried my best to check existing issues ### Repro ExplorerPatcher versions ExplorerPatcher version: 26100.8457.70.3 ### Repro Windows Versions Windows 11 25H2 26200.8655 x64 ### Third-party tweak software installed None known ### Describe the bug 1. Configure the taskbar to be positioned on the left side of the main screen. 2. Enable auto-hide for the taskbar. 3. Click a tray/taskbar application icon that opens a popup window, for example OneDrive. 4. Observe the position of the popup. ### Expected outcome The popup should be fully visible and should not be covered by the taskbar. ### Actual outcome The popup appears behind of the taskbar, and some part of it is obstructed. ### Additional info This may be related to existing left-side auto-hide positioning issues such as #4510 / #1782, where Start/Search flyouts were positioned behind the taskbar. However, in this case the affected popup is a third-party tray application popup, for example OneDrive. It may involve the notification-area icon rectangle / flyout anchor rectangle used by ep_taskbar. The popup appears correctly, if auto-hide is turned off. ### Crash Dumps _No response_ ### Media

title: Windows 10 taskbar: Taskbar tooltips occasionally remain stuck on screen after mouse leaves taskbar (Vertical taskbar)
author: thuggs
body: ### Before reporting your issue - [x] I have confirmed that this issue does not happen when ExplorerPatcher is not installed - [x] I do not have "register as shell extension" enabled - [x] I have tried my best to check existing issues ### Repro ExplorerPatcher versions ExplorerPatcher 70.3 ### Repro Windows Versions Windows 11 24H2 (26100.8655) ### Third-party tweak software installed None ### Describe the bug Occasionally, taskbar tooltips remain visible after moving the mouse away from a taskbar button. Instead of disappearing normally, the tooltip remains permanently on the desktop until another event causes it to disappear (for example, hovering another taskbar item or restarting Explorer). This happens with all applications, not just one specific application. Restarting File Explorer temporarily fixes the issue. **Example:** Hover Outlook taskbar button → tooltip appears normally. Move mouse away from the taskbar. Tooltip remains visible instead of disappearing. **Frequency- Intermittent.** It occurs regularly enough to be noticeable during normal daily use. Additional information I have experienced this across multiple ExplorerPatcher releases. I have experienced this across multiple Windows 11 versions. It is not new to 70.3. I always use the Windows 10 ExplorerPatcher taskbar because I use a left-side vertical taskbar, so I have not tested with the Windows 11 taskbar implementation. I suspect it may be related to the ExplorerPatcher Windows 10 taskbar implementation, particularly when using a vertical taskbar. ### Expected outcome **Expected behavior** The tooltip should disappear immediately after the cursor leaves the taskbar button. ### Actual outcome **Actual behavior** The tooltip occasionally remains visible indefinitely after the mouse leaves the taskbar button. ### Additional info First, I did use AI to help share the issue on paper. **Environment** Windows 11 24H2 (26100.8655) ExplorerPatcher 70.3 Taskbar style: Windows 10 (ExplorerPatcher) Primary taskbar location: Left Taskbar icon size: Small Combine taskbar icons: Never combine ### Crash Dumps _No response_ ### Media

title: Search box disappears after clicking it on Windows 10 taskbar (Windows 11 25H2 Build 26200.8653)
author: AlexGo12311
body: ### Before reporting your issue - [ ] I have confirmed that this issue does not happen when ExplorerPatcher is not installed - [ ] I do not have "register as shell extension" enabled - [ ] I have tried my best to check existing issues ### Repro ExplorerPatcher versions Confirmed on 26100.8457.70.3. ### Repro Windows Versions Windows 11 25H2 (Build 26200.8653) ### Third-party tweak software installed No. Stock Windows. Newly installed ### Describe the bug ### Environment - Windows 11 25H2 - OS Build: 26200.8653 - ExplorerPatcher: 26100.8457.70.3 ### Description When using the Windows 10 taskbar in ExplorerPatcher, the Search Box disappears after it is clicked. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Enable the Windows 10 taskbar. 2. Set Taskbar Search to "Search Box". 3. Click on the search box. ### Expected behavior The search UI should open while the search box remains visible on the taskbar. ### Actual behavior The search box disappears from the taskbar immediately after being clicked. The search functionality itself still works: - Win + S works normally. - The Search icon mode also works correctly. - Only the Search Box mode is affected. ### Additional information The issue persists after restarting Explorer and rebooting the system. ### Expected outcome The Search Box should remain visible after being clicked and open the search interface normally, just like it does when using the Search icon. Clicking the Search Box should not cause it to disappear from the taskbar. ### Actual outcome Clicking the Search Box causes it to disappear from the Windows 10 taskbar. The search functionality itself still works (Win + S and the Search icon mode work normally), but the Search Box is removed from the taskbar until Explorer is restarted or the setting is changed. ### Additional info _No response_ ### Crash Dumps _No response_ ### Media

title: Folder Renaming Issue in File Explorer
author: jozimk
body: ### Before reporting your issue - [x] I have confirmed that this issue does not happen when ExplorerPatcher is not installed - [x] I do not have "register as shell extension" enabled - [x] I have tried my best to check existing issues ### Repro ExplorerPatcher versions Explorer Patcher Version 26100.8457.70.3 ### Repro Windows Versions Windows 11 Home SL Version 25H2 OS Build 26200.8737 ### Third-party tweak software installed - Chris Titus Tech WinUtil (Used solely to disable telemetry and to enable legacy file context menus) - O&O Shutup 10 - MS Edge Redirect Tool (Insignificant, Installed from above two utilities) - (Previously) Microsoft PowerToys (Before installing EP) - No antivirus software in use Please note that after I detected this issue I disabled all previous tweaks that modified my system using these three utilities, while still keeping EP installed. There is no change in bug, it still persists. While keeping tweaks disabled, I uninstalled EP and the bug dissappeared. Folder renaming worked as usual. ### Describe the bug - Installed EP to enable Windows 10 Legacy Ribbon Format in File Explorer and Start Menus and Taskbar. - Renaming of folder **(only folders, not files)** using the legacy context menu brings up an error that says: (Window Name: "Rename Folder") - "Can't find the specified file. Make sure you specify the correct path and file name. Date Created: ." (Screenshot appended) - When [Try Again] button is selected (Window Name:"Rename") "The file or folder does not exist." - The title reverts to the original, and no data in the folder is affected. - This same dialog and error message is obtained when the folder is renamed via the Properties option and menu box. - However, folder renmaing still works without any issue using the Command Prompt and PowerShell. - File renaming works as usual with no glitches. Only Folders are affected. ### Expected outcome Standard Rename Process for Folders. ### Actual outcome The folder rename issue occurs across all drives, including the system drive, flash drives, and network drives (SMB hosted on the local HDD). The issue is very consistent, strongly indicating an underlying issue with EP. Once during testing, I briefly enabled "Register as Shell Extension" to check whether the bug disappeared (Before the uninstall as mentioned earlier). The bug still occured. Also, after disabling that and refreshing, for the first time ever, WIndows Explorer crashed heavily and I had to Ctrl+Shift+Esc to manually restart it myself. This however, only happened once. ### Additional info While this issue was mentioned before, it was on Win10, and it applied to all files, so i thought I'd report this myself. Attached is an export of the current EP settings I used. Disclaimer : Please note that it was initally a .reg file. In order to be able to upload the exact file itself I renamed it to .txt extension. The text file doesn not seem to be corrupted in any way. Note on Context Menu Tweak: The legacy context menu tweak from WinUtil modifies the `InprocServer32` key under `CLSID\{86ca1aa0-34aa-4e8b-a509-50c905bae2a2}` . Even though I disabled it during testing, please check if ExplorerPatcher's Ribbon is conflicting with previous registry entries (especially for folders). i'm not sure with this. [ExplorerPatcher_26100.8457.70.3.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29661303/ExplorerPatcher_26100.8457.70.3.txt) ### Crash Dumps No dump data could be found. ### Media Dialog Box and Error Message. Screenshots. All New Folders created are stuck on "New Folder". Folders with unique names cannot be altered. In the appended image "New Folder" was tried to be renamed to "Test" in the Downloads Folder. EP was enabled.

Have not had it happen when restart, when logging off - then on again. Only from full shutdown -> startup.

expect to start normally (IE not have to restart explorer to continue to windows)

### Actual outcome

As explained, when using task manager to restart explorer, windows starts up as expected.

title: Make ExplorerPatcher Shut Up about crashs because it's still working
author: jabcreations
body:
### Before reporting your issue

- [x] I have confirmed that this issue does not happen when ExplorerPatcher is not installed
- [x] I do not have "register as shell extension" enabled
- [x] I have tried my best to check existing issues

### Repro ExplorerPatcher versions

Some clients have their computers infected with Windows 11 and so part of restoring a usable GUI is using ExplorerPatcher. The thing is: it keeps complaining that it crashes, _all the time_. If it crashed the Open-Shell start menu fix would be at the center of the screen however it's still at the bottom-left where it belongs.

Having a crash notice appear all of the time (except literally never when I'm viewing their computer) is really starting to annoy my clients many of whom have no idea the nightmare normal users like them have to deal with when they don't have a competent technical professional like me helping them. That being said we've got things to do. Please give us a simple option to make ExplorerPatcher shut up about crashes. They are either not actually happening or not having any actual effect but the error messages are the only actual problem.

### Repro Windows Versions

Client PC not accessible at this time.

### Third-party tweak software installed

OpenShell, Waterfox, AnyDesk

### Describe the bug

.

### Expected outcome

To not be obnoxious while working.

### Actual outcome

Working but being obnoxious.

### Additional info

_No response_

### Crash Dumps

_No response_

### Media

_No response_

title: After a certain time that the display went off automatically, on resume of activity the maximised windows are moved all the way up (under top taskbar).
author: AlterNate37
body:
### Before reporting your issue

- [x] I have confirmed that this issue does not happen when ExplorerPatcher is not installed
- [x] I do not have "register as shell extension" enabled
- [x] I have tried my best to check existing issues

### Repro ExplorerPatcher versions

26100.8457.70.3

### Repro Windows Versions

Win 11 Pro 25H2 Build 26200.8655

### Third-party tweak software installed

WinSetView (I use it to change the columns shown in several categories of File Explorer like Documents, Downloads, Music etc)
O&O Shutup10 (mostly changes privacy settings but it has some options to hide Teams, Cortana, Search etc from Taskbar)

Tho, considering that the same issue is described in other bug-reports where these two software weren't named, I'd guess they aren't involved.


### Describe the bug

_Pre-requisite_: taskbar on top.

_Steps:_
- I leave the system for a while.
- When I come back the display is off. The PC might or might not have entered S0 sleep (sorry, I wasn't able to determine that with certainty).
- I resume activity, display goes on.


### Expected outcome

Everything should be like I left it, with the maximised windows filling the whole available screen, their upper border aligned with the taskbar's bottom border.


### Actual outcome

The maximised windows are now moved up, with their upper part hidden under the taskbar, and underneath them a gap equal to the thickness of the taskbar, from which the desktop can be seen.


### Additional info

- If I pull the bottom border of the taskbar down, making the taskbar thicker, the upper part of the windows unhides and becomes again aligned with the taskbar, and the windows automatically fill the whole screen, closing the previously mentioned gap at the bottom.

- Some apps, like Perplexity and Copilot, open directly with the upper part hidden under the taskbar.
If I pull their bottom border all the way down, the window expands and the previously hidden part is again aligned with the taskbar.

- The issue happens both on my laptop's integrated display and on the external one.

- The issue doesn't happen when I put the system to sleep manually.

- The issue doesn't happen when I wake up the system shortly after it automatically went to sleep (i.e. I tried setting the sleep time to 1 minute so I don't have to wait ages, and I woke it after 1-2 minutes and there was no anomaly. I therefore suppose that the issue is related to something that happens only after a certain time that the display goes off, and/or that the system goes to sleep.)
 
- The issue can be reproduced by reinitialising the graphics stack with Ctrl+Shift+Win+B, like the OP of report #4058 said.
Unlike that report's OP tho, I can't say if this implies a cause-effect relationship.

- No idea if relevant but I have an integrated Intel Iris Xe Graphics. 
I mention that because the issue's apparent relationship with the reset/restart/reinitialisation of graphic drivers makes me wonder if maybe it only happens with integrated (Intel?) Graphics.
